text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
Date of Birth: [REDACTED]
Attending Surgeon: [REDACTED]
Ordering Provider: [REDACTED]
Service: Gynecologic Oncology

SPECIMEN RECEIVED:
  A. Right ovary and fallopian tube
  B. Left ovary and fallopian tube
  C. Uterus with cervix
  D. Omentum (infracolic)
  E. Omentum (supracolic)
  F. Right diaphragm peritoneal strip
  G. Right paracolic gutter peritoneum
  H. Rectosigmoid colon segment
  I. Spleen with hilar soft tissue
  J. Appendix
  K. Gallbladder
  L. Right pelvic lymph nodes
  M. Left pelvic lymph nodes
  N. Right para-aortic lymph nodes
  O. Left para-aortic lymph nodes

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
TYPE OF PROCEDURE: Primary debulking surgery (PDS) — total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy (infracolic and supracolic), bilateral pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, peritoneal stripping bilateral diaphragmatic surfaces, right paracolic peritonectomy, rectosigmoid resection with primary colorectal anastomosis, appendectomy, splenectomy, cholecystectomy
CLINICAL HISTORY: 58-year-old woman with known FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the ovary/fallopian tube. Bilateral ovarian masses, omental cake, peritoneal carcinomatosis on preoperative imaging. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC) on record. Proceeding with upfront cytoreductive surgery.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Specimen A — Right ovary and fallopian tube:
Received fresh then placed in formalin. The specimen consists of an ovary measuring 8.4 x 7.1 x 5.8 cm with attached fallopian tube 9.5 cm in length x 0.7 cm in diameter. The external ovarian surface is extensively disrupted with papillary excrescences and areas of hemorrhagic rupture over approximately 60% of the surface. Cut section reveals a predominantly solid tan-white mass with focal necrotic and hemorrhagic areas occupying the majority of the ovarian parenchyma; residual normal ovarian stroma is not identified. The fallopian tube is serially sectioned; the fimbriated end is identifiable and submitted in its entirety per SEE-FIM protocol. The tubal mucosa shows irregular thickening of the fimbriated end with a raised plaque-like lesion measuring 0.4 x 0.3 cm. No intraluminal filling defects. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: A1-A4 (ovarian mass, representative), A5-A6 (ovarian surface with excrescences), A7-A10 (fallopian tube, full-face fimbriated end per SEE-FIM), A11-A12 (proximal and mid tube).

Specimen B — Left ovary and fallopian tube:
Received in formalin. The specimen consists of an ovary measuring 5.1 x 4.3 x 3.9 cm with attached fallopian tube 8.8 cm in length x 0.6 cm diameter. The external ovarian surface shows nodular papillary excrescences over approximately 40% of the surface with focal disruption. Cut section reveals a predominantly solid to focally cystic tan-yellow mass with areas of chalky necrosis. The cystic areas contain turbid yellow-brown fluid. The fallopian tube is serially sectioned per SEE-FIM protocol. The fimbriated end shows no grossly apparent lesion. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: B1-B3 (ovarian mass), B4-B5 (ovarian surface), B6-B9 (fallopian tube fimbriated end, full-face), B10-B11 (proximal tube).

Specimen C — Uterus with cervix:
Received fresh then fixed. The uterus weighs 87 grams and measures 8.2 x 5.3 x 4.1 cm. The serosal surface is tan-pink and shows multiple firm white nodular implants ranging from 0.2 to 1.1 cm across the fundal and posterior serosal surfaces; the largest implant measures 1.1 x 0.9 cm on the posterior uterine fundus. The anterior serosa is relatively unremarkable. On bivalving, the endometrial cavity measures 4.5 cm in depth; the endometrial lining is thin, atrophic, measuring less than 1 mm without grossly apparent lesion. The myometrium is homogeneous and tan-white, 2.1 cm in thickness at the fundus, without discrete mass or infiltration. The cervix is unremarkable, os patent. No adnexal tissue present (submitted separately). Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: C1-C2 (anterior myometrium and endometrium), C3-C4 (posterior myometrium and endometrium), C5-C7 (serosal implants, posterior fundus), C8 (cervix, anterior lip), C9 (cervix, posterior lip).

Specimen D — Omentum (infracolic):
Received in formalin. A large irregular fibrofatty tissue mass measuring 28 x 18 x 4.5 cm and weighing 312 grams — consistent with "omental cake." The external surface and cut sections are diffusely replaced by confluent firm white-tan tumor nodules forming a near-continuous sheet of carcinoma throughout the tissue. Focal areas of central necrosis are present. Residual adipose tissue is minimal. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: D1-D6 (representative sections including areas of necrosis and viable tumor).

Specimen E — Omentum (supracolic):
Received in formalin. A fibrofatty tissue fragment measuring 14 x 10 x 2.0 cm. Cut sections show multiple discrete firm white tumor nodules ranging from 0.3 to 2.8 cm scattered throughout the adipose tissue. The largest nodule measures 2.8 x 2.2 cm and shows central necrosis. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: E1-E3 (representative nodules).

Specimen F — Right diaphragm peritoneal strip:
Received in formalin. Multiple irregular peritoneal and fibromuscular tissue fragments, in aggregate measuring 9.5 x 7.0 x 1.5 cm. The peritoneal surface shows multifocal firm white nodular implants ranging from 0.2 to 1.4 cm. The largest implant measures 1.4 x 1.0 cm. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: F1-F4 (representative implants and peritoneal surface).

Specimen G — Right paracolic gutter peritoneum:
Received in formalin. Irregular fibrofatty peritoneal tissue fragments aggregating 6.0 x 4.5 x 1.0 cm. Multiple discrete firm white nodular implants are present ranging from 0.2 to 0.9 cm. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: G1-G2 (representative implants).

Specimen H — Rectosigmoid colon segment:
Received fresh then fixed. A segment of rectosigmoid colon measuring 18 cm in length. The mesenteric margin is inked. The serosal and subserosal surfaces show confluent white-tan tumor infiltration over a segment approximately 7 cm in length beginning 4 cm from the proximal margin. The tumor has a firm, white, infiltrative gross appearance consistent with carcinoma involving the serosa and subserosa. The mucosa is carefully examined on opening and appears grossly unremarkable — pink-tan, with intact folds and no ulceration or mucosal involvement identified. The bowel lumen is patent. Proximal resection margin is grossly free of tumor (nearest tumor 4 cm). Distal resection margin is grossly free of tumor (nearest tumor 3.5 cm). Radial mesenteric margin is inked and assessed. No obvious lymph nodes are identified in the mesentery on gross exam (all tissue submitted). Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: H1 (proximal resection margin, en face), H2 (distal resection margin, en face), H3-H6 (tumor with serosal and subserosal involvement, perpendicular to wall), H7-H8 (additional tumor, mesenteric margin), H9 (grossly uninvolved colon, representative).

Specimen I — Spleen with hilar soft tissue:
Received fresh then fixed. The spleen weighs 183 grams and measures 12.5 x 8.0 x 4.0 cm. The capsular surface is smooth and unremarkable. At the splenic hilum, there is a firm white tumor nodule measuring 3.2 x 2.5 x 2.0 cm embedded in the perisplenic hilar soft tissue; this nodule is partially adherent to the splenic capsule but the splenic parenchyma is intact on cut section. Cut section of the spleen shows a homogeneous red-brown parenchyma without discrete mass lesion, necrosis, or additional tumor nodules. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: I1-I3 (hilar tumor nodule, including interface with splenic capsule), I4 (splenic parenchyma adjacent to hilum), I5 (splenic parenchyma away from hilum, representative).

Specimen J — Appendix:
Received in formalin. A vermiform appendix measuring 6.5 cm in length x 0.6 cm in diameter. The serosal surface is tan-pink and smooth without tumor implants. The lumen contains inspissated material. The wall appears unremarkable. Tip and cross sections submitted.
  Cassettes: J1 (tip, cross-sections x3; proximal cross-section).

Specimen K — Gallbladder:
Received in formalin. The gallbladder measures 8.5 x 3.5 x 2.5 cm. The serosal surface is smooth and glistening. The wall measures 0.2 cm. The mucosa is green and velvety. The lumen contains multiple yellow-green faceted gallstones ranging from 0.3 to 1.1 cm (approximately 8 stones). No mass, polyp, or tumor implant identified. Representative sections submitted.
  Cassettes: K1 (fundus, body, cystic duct margin).

Specimen L — Right pelvic lymph nodes:
Received in formalin. Multiple tan-gray soft tissue fragments containing lymph nodes; 10 lymph nodes identified, ranging from 0.4 to 2.1 cm. Several nodes appear grossly enlarged and replaced by white firm tumor deposits. The largest grossly involved node measures 2.1 x 1.8 cm. All nodes submitted entirely.
  Cassettes: L1-L5 (lymph nodes, all submitted).

Specimen M — Left pelvic lymph nodes:
Received in formalin. Multiple tan-gray soft tissue fragments; 8 lymph nodes identified, ranging from 0.3 to 1.4 cm. No grossly apparent tumor involvement. All nodes submitted entirely.
  Cassettes: M1-M3 (lymph nodes, all submitted).

Specimen N — Right para-aortic lymph nodes:
Received in formalin. Multiple lymph node-bearing fibrofatty tissue fragments; 8 lymph nodes identified ranging from 0.3 to 1.9 cm. One node (1.9 cm) appears partially replaced by firm white tumor. All nodes submitted entirely.
  Cassettes: N1-N3 (lymph nodes, all submitted).

Specimen O — Left para-aortic lymph nodes:
Received in formalin. Multiple lymph node-bearing fibrofatty tissue fragments; 6 lymph nodes identified ranging from 0.3 to 1.2 cm. No grossly apparent tumor involvement. All nodes submitted entirely.
  Cassettes: O1-O2 (lymph nodes, all submitted).

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

Specimen A — Right ovary and fallopian tube:
The ovarian parenchyma is replaced by sheets and nests of highly atypical epithelial cells with marked nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm, and numerous mitotic figures including atypical forms. Papillary and micropapillary growth patterns are present focally. Psammoma bodies are identified. Extensive tumor necrosis is noted. The ovarian surface is breached by carcinoma. The fallopian tube shows a focus of atypical serous proliferation confined to the mucosal epithelium of the fimbriated end, with p53 aberrant nuclear staining and increased Ki-67 proliferation index — consistent with serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC). The STIC is sharply demarcated from adjacent normal tubal epithelium. The remainder of the tubal epithelium is unremarkable. There is no invasive carcinoma within the tubal wall.

Specimen B — Left ovary and fallopian tube:
Similar high-grade serous carcinoma as described in Specimen A, involving the majority of the ovarian parenchyma with surface extension. The fallopian tube fimbriated end mucosa shows reactive changes but no STIC or invasive carcinoma.

Specimen C — Uterus with cervix:
The serosal surface shows multiple deposits of high-grade serous carcinoma identical in morphology to the ovarian primaries. The underlying myometrium and endometrium are uninvolved. The endometrium is atrophic. The cervix shows no significant pathology; no dysplasia or carcinoma.

Specimen D & E — Omentum (infracolic and supracolic):
Both specimens show extensive infiltration by high-grade serous carcinoma forming confluent sheets and nodules with central necrosis. Fibrous stroma with lymphocytic infiltrate is present focally.

Specimen F — Right diaphragm peritoneal strip:
Multifocal deposits of high-grade serous carcinoma on the peritoneal surface and within the fibromuscular tissue.

Specimen G — Right paracolic gutter peritoneum:
Multifocal serosal implants of high-grade serous carcinoma.

Specimen H — Rectosigmoid colon segment:
High-grade serous carcinoma infiltrates the serosa and subserosa over the segment noted grossly. The muscularis propria shows focal superficial involvement. The mucosa is uninvolved. Both resection margins are negative for carcinoma. The mesenteric soft tissue margin is negative.

Specimen I — Spleen with hilar soft tissue:
The hilar soft tissue nodule consists of metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma. The splenic parenchyma is uninvolved; the carcinoma is limited to the perisplenic soft tissue with focal adherence to the capsule without parenchymal penetration.

Specimen J — Appendix:
No tumor involvement. Mild periappendiceal fibrosis. No acute appendicitis.

Specimen K — Gallbladder:
Cholesterol gallstones. Mild chronic cholecystitis. No tumor involvement.

Specimen L — Right pelvic lymph nodes:
Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 3 of 10 lymph nodes (3/10). The largest metastatic deposit measures 1.4 cm (present in the 2.1 cm node) with extranodal extension into perinodal soft tissue.

Specimen M — Left pelvic lymph nodes:
Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 1 of 8 lymph nodes (1/8). The metastatic deposit measures 0.7 cm without extranodal extension.

Specimen N — Right para-aortic lymph nodes:
Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 2 of 8 lymph nodes (2/8). Largest deposit 1.1 cm; extranodal extension present in one node.

Specimen O — Left para-aortic lymph nodes:
No tumor in 6 lymph nodes examined (0/6).

Total lymph node involvement: 6 of 32 nodes (4 pelvic + 2 para-aortic, per aggregate count as recorded — see comment).

Comment: The total lymph node count across all nodal stations is 32 nodes examined, with 6 positive (18.75%). The primary site of origin is felt to be the right fallopian tube based on the presence of STIC in the right fimbriated end with absent STIC in the left tube, consistent with current criteria for tubal primary in the setting of bilateral ovarian/tubo-ovarian high-grade serous carcinoma. The designation "ovarian/fallopian tube/primary peritoneal" is applied per FIGO/SGO convention.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

Immunohistochemistry (performed on Specimen A, right ovary, with concordant results verified on representative specimens as noted):

  PAX8: Strongly and diffusely positive (nuclear) in tumor cells — supports Mullerian/gynecologic primary.
  WT-1: Strongly and diffusely positive (nuclear) in tumor cells — supports serous carcinoma of gynecologic origin.
  p53: Aberrant pattern — diffuse strong overexpression ("wild-type-like" clonal overexpression pattern) in >80% of tumor cell nuclei, consistent with gain-of-function TP53 mutation. (See note: prior molecular studies document somatic TP53 mutation.)
  p16: Diffuse block positivity.
  ER: Focally positive (~20% of tumor cells, weak-moderate).
  PR: Negative.
  Ki-67 (MIB-1): Proliferation index approximately 65–70%.
  HER2 (4B5): 1+ (incomplete membrane staining in <10% of tumor cells) — negative by standard criteria.
  Napsin A: Negative.
  CK7: Positive.
  CK20: Negative.
  CDX2: Negative.
  TTF-1: Negative.

SEE-FIM (Sectioning and Extensively Examining the Fimbriated End) Protocol, Specimen A right fallopian tube:
  STIC confirmed as described above. p53 staining: aberrant (strong diffuse nuclear overexpression). Ki-67: markedly elevated in STIC focus (~60%) versus adjacent normal tubal epithelium (<5%).

Molecular/Additional Studies:
  BRCA1/2 germline testing: Results on record — germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant c.5266dupC (5382insC) previously documented from separate germline testing. Not repeated on this specimen.
  Mismatch repair (MMR) IHC: MLH1, PMS2, MSH2, MSH6 — all intact (proficient MMR). [Results pending at time of preliminary sign-out; results incorporated into final report.]

---

FINAL DIAGNOSES

A. RIGHT OVARY AND FALLOPIAN TUBE:
   - High-grade serous carcinoma, right ovary, 8.4 cm; ovarian surface involved by carcinoma.
   - Serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC), right fallopian tube fimbriated end; no invasive carcinoma within fallopian tube wall.
   - Consistent with fallopian tube as site of origin.

B. LEFT OVARY AND FALLOPIAN TUBE:
   - High-grade serous carcinoma, left ovary, 5.1 cm; ovarian surface involved.
   - Left fallopian tube: No STIC; no invasive carcinoma.

C. UTERUS WITH CERVIX:
   - Serosal implants of high-grade serous carcinoma, multifocal.
   - Endometrium and myometrium: Uninvolved.
   - Cervix: No significant pathology.

D. OMENTUM (INFRACOLIC):
   - Extensive involvement by metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma (omental cake).

E. OMENTUM (SUPRACOLIC):
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma, multifocal.

F. RIGHT DIAPHRAGM PERITONEAL STRIP:
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma, multifocal.

G. RIGHT PARACOLIC GUTTER PERITONEUM:
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma, multifocal.

H. RECTOSIGMOID COLON SEGMENT:
   - Serosal and subserosal involvement by metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma.
   - Mucosa: Uninvolved.
   - Proximal resection margin: Negative.
   - Distal resection margin: Negative.
   - Mesenteric radial margin: Negative.

I. SPLEEN WITH HILAR SOFT TISSUE:
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma, splenic hilar soft tissue, 3.2 cm.
   - Splenic parenchyma: Uninvolved.

J. APPENDIX:
   - No tumor. Mild periappendiceal fibrosis.

K. GALLBLADDER:
   - Cholesterol cholelithiasis. Mild chronic cholecystitis. No tumor.

L. RIGHT PELVIC LYMPH NODES:
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 3 of 10 lymph nodes (3/10).
   - Extranodal extension: Present.

M. LEFT PELVIC LYMPH NODES:
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 1 of 8 lymph nodes (1/8).
   - Extranodal extension: Absent.

N. RIGHT PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES:
   - Metastatic high-grade serous carcinoma in 2 of 8 lymph nodes (2/8).
   - Extranodal extension: Present in 1 node.

O. LEFT PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES:
   - No tumor in 6 lymph nodes (0/6).

---

FINAL PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Ed. / FIGO 2014):
   Primary site: Fallopian tube (STIC confirmed right fimbriated end)
   pT3c — Peritoneal metastases beyond pelvis, > 2 cm; includes splenic and diaphragmatic serosal involvement
   pN1 (pN1a) — Regional lymph node metastases (pelvic and para-aortic)
   pM0 — No distant metastasis identified in surgical specimen
   FIGO Stage IIIC

Residual disease at completion of cytoreduction (as reported by surgeon): R0 — no gross residual disease.

---

Pathologist: [REDACTED], M.D.
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Electronic signature on file.
Preliminary diagnosis communicated to surgical team intraoperatively.
Final report issued: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
